title: Team Management in CloudDash
content:
CloudDash team management features help organizations structure members into teams, control access, and maintain accountability across monitoring operations.

Organization Structure: Every CloudDash account belongs to an organization. Organizations contain teams (optional groupings) and members (individual users). A member can belong to multiple teams. Teams enable scoped dashboard sharing and alert ownership.

Creating Teams: Settings > Team Management > Teams > Create Team. Name the team (e.g., 'Platform Engineering', 'Data Team', 'On-Call Rotation') and add members. Teams do not affect billing — seat counts are per organization, not per team.

Member Lifecycle: Invite via email (Settings > Team Management > Invite), accept invitation, active use, deactivation, or removal. Deactivated members retain their account but lose organization access. Removed members lose all access immediately. Their created resources remain unless explicitly deleted.

Team-Based Dashboard Sharing: When creating or editing a dashboard, set Visibility to 'Team' and select the target team. Only team members see the dashboard in their list. Organization-wide dashboards remain visible to all members regardless of team membership.

On-Call Integration: Assign on-call schedules to teams using CloudDash's built-in scheduling (Pro+) or PagerDuty integration. Alert rules can route notifications to the current on-call member of a specific team.

Ownership Transfer: When removing an Admin or Editor, CloudDash prompts to reassign ownership of their dashboards, alert rules, and integrations. Unassigned resources are transferred to the removing Admin. Bulk ownership transfer is available under Team Management > Transfer Resources.

SCIM Provisioning (Enterprise): Automate member creation, deactivation, and team assignment via SCIM 2.0 with Okta, Azure AD, or custom SCIM clients. Configure under Settings > Security > SCIM. SCIM-managed members show a 'Provisioned' badge in Team Management.
